Effects: Flip a coin for each Energy attached to this Pokémon. This attack does 70 damage for each heads.
Flavor-Text: They flare their frills and generate energy. A single Heliolisk can generate sufficient electricity to power a skyscraper.

Name: Vikavolt
Effects: Switch this Pokémon with 1 of your Benched [L] Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: When carrying a Charjabug, Vikavolt can receive electricity from it and then rapidly fire powerful electromagnetic beams from its large jaws.

Name: Iono’s Wattrel
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 20 more damage.
Flavor-Text: When its wings catch the wind, the bones within produce electricity. This Pokémon dives into the ocean, catching prey by electrocuting them.

Name: Marill
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, during your opponent’s next turn, prevent all damage from and effects of attacks done to this Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: The oil-filled tail functions as a buoy, so it’s fine even in rivers with strong currents.

Name: Misdreavus
Effects: Search your deck for a card that evolves from this Pokémon and put it onto this Pokémon to evolve it. Then, shuffle your deck.
Flavor-Text: It loves to bite and yank people’s hair from behind without warning, just to see their shocked reactions.

Name: Banette
Effects: Your opponent chooses 3 cards from their hand and shuffles those cards into their deck.
Flavor-Text: This Pokémon developed from an abandoned doll that amassed a grudge. It is seen in dark alleys.

Name: Togekiss
Ability-Effect: When your opponent’s Active Pokémon is Knocked Out, flip a coin. If heads, take 1 more Prize card. The effect of Wonder Kiss doesn’t stack.
Flavor-Text: These Pokémon are never seen anywhere near conflict or turmoil. In recent times, they’ve hardly been seen at all.

Name: Slurpuff
Flavor-Text: It can distinguish the faintest of scents. It puts its sensitive sense of smell to use by helping pastry chefs in their work.

Name: Hop’s Trevenant
Effects: If any of your Hop’s Pokémon were Knocked Out by damage from an attack during your opponent’s last turn, this attack does 100 more damage.
During your opponent’s next turn, the Defending Pokémon can’t retreat.
Flavor-Text: Using its roots as a nervous system, it controls the trees in the forest. It’s kind to the Pokémon that reside in its body.

Name: Team Rocket’s Mimikyu
Effects: Choose 1 of your opponent’s Active Tera Pokémon’s attacks and use it as this attack.
Flavor-Text: This Pokémon lives in dark places untouched by sunlight. When it appears before humans, it hides itself under a cloth that resembles a Pikachu.

Name: Team Rocket’s Dugtrio
Ability-Effect: Whenever your opponent’s Active Pokémon moves to the Bench during their turn, place 2 damage counters on that Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: In battle, it digs through the ground and strikes the unsuspecting foe from an unexpected direction.

Name: Hitmontop
Effects: Shuffle your hand into your deck. Then, draw 6 cards.
Flavor-Text: It spins on its horn while dealing out elegant kicks. Its horn grows continuously through its lifetime.

Name: Medicham
Effects: If you don’t have exactly 7 cards in your hand, this attack does nothing.
Flavor-Text: Through yoga training, it gained the psychic power to predict its foe’s movements.

Name: Carbink
Ability-Effect: As long as this Pokémon is in play, it is [F] and [P] type.
Effects: If your opponent has 2 or fewer Prize cards remaining, this attack does 100 more damage.
Flavor-Text: Born from the high temperatures and pressures deep underground, it releases energy from the stone in its head.

Name: Mightyena
Effects: Switch out your opponent’s Active Pokémon to the Bench. (Your opponent chooses the new Active Pokémon.)
Flavor-Text: It will always obey the commands of a skilled Trainer. Its behavior arises from its living in packs in ancient times.

Name: Cynthia’s Spiritomb
Effects: This attack does 10 damage for each damage counter on all of your Benched Cynthia’s Pokémon. This attack’s damage isn’t affected by Weakness.
Flavor-Text: Its constant mischief and misdeeds resulted in it being bound to an Odd Keystone by a mysterious spell.

Name: Galarian Obstagoon
Effects: This attack does 70 damage for each damage counter on your opponent’s Active Pokémon.
Discard an Energy from this Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: Its voice is staggering in volume. Obstagoon has a tendency to take on a threatening posture and shout—this move is known as Obstruct.

Name: Mawile
Effects: Search your deck for up to 2 Basic Pokémon and put them onto your Bench. Then, shuffle your deck.
Flavor-Text: Attached to its head is a huge set of jaws formed by horns. It can chew through iron beams.

Name: Dreepy
Flavor-Text: It has a habit of biting at Clauncher even though it doesn’t feed on them. This is said to be vestigial behavior from when Dreepy was alive.

Name: Drakloak
Ability-Effect: Once during your turn, you may look at the top 2 cards of your deck and put 1 of them into your hand. Put the other card on the bottom of your deck.
Flavor-Text: This Pokémon stores up energy in its lungs, then shoots it out. It takes care of Dreepy and battles alongside them until they’re all grown up.

Name: Larry’s Staraptor
Effects: If this Pokémon is Burned or Poisoned, this attack does 100 more damage.
Discard 2 Energy from this Pokémon, and this attack also does 50 damage to 1 of your opponent’s Benched Pokémon. (Don’t apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)
Flavor-Text: It never stops attacking even if it is injured. It fusses over the shape of its comb.

Name: Fan Rotom
Ability-Effect: Once during your first turn, you may search your deck for up to 3 [C] Pokémon with 100 HP or less, reveal them, and put them into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck. You can’t use more than 1 Fan Call Ability during your turn.
Effects: If there is no Stadium in play, this attack does nothing.
Flavor-Text: There are several appliances that Rotom can inspirit, but the first one to be developed was the electric fan.

ex-Rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

ex-Rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.
